title: ictodes foetidus
intro: Ictodes foetidus, also known as the stinking iris or stink iris, is a plant species in the family Iridaceae. The roots and rhizomes of Ictodes foetidus contain iridoid glycosides, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, fever, and rheumatism, due to its antiseptic and analgesic properties. Ictodes foetidus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems and as a remedy for snake bites.
